mitigations: Short-haul route (2hr, 956km) limits fuel exposure per flight
Thai AirAsia still operating CXR-DMK 7x weekly (FD647) as of Mar 31 -- no cancellation announced yet
SEA airspace completely unaffected by Middle East closures
13 days until departure -- rebooked to April 13, ahead of the projected April 10-15 fuel shipment window, reducing exposure to further schedule cuts
Bangkok is a critical hub; carriers have strong commercial incentive to maintain CXR-DMK even under reduced schedules

Expect 90 min to 3 hours additional flight time via southern (Egypt-Saudi-Oman) or northern (Caucasus) corridor
Brent crude at $112.78/bbl (Mar 30-31), Goldman Sachs estimates $14-18 geopolitical risk premium baked in

Thai Airways raised ticket prices 10-15% to offset fuel costs; further surcharges possible if crude rises further

BKK-CPH direct route still operating with rerouting and minimal cancellations (74 delays, 0 cancellations reported)

fuelOil.summary: Brent crude at $112.78/bbl (Mar 30-31), highest since July 2022. SEA Jet A-1 at $234.3/bbl, roughly 2.5x pre-war levels. Goldman Sachs estimates $14-18/bbl geopolitical risk premium in current prices. No relief in sight with Iran-US talks broken off and Strait of Hormuz still effectively closed. Jet fuel up 82.8% month-on-month per IATA.
details: Jet A-1 at SEA hubs holding at $234.3/bbl -- up from $95/bbl in late February (147% increase)
IATA jet fuel price monitor: $197/bbl for week ending Mar 20, suggesting further increases in final March week
China and Thailand fuel export bans still in effect, directly cutting Vietnam's supply with no stated end date
Vietnam fuel delivery timelines extended from 30 to 50+ days
Oil market in backwardation, signaling continued supply tightness through Q2 2026
Vietnam Airlines planning 10-20% monthly flight cuts next quarter if jet fuel stays above $160-200/bbl
Asia's air travel crisis now spreading toward Europe per Bloomberg/Japan Times (Mar 27-28)
Regional aviation fuel at SEA hubs: $1.89-$2.12 per liter, a 47% increase vs January 2026
Jet fuel surged 82.8% month-on-month and 94.4% year-on-year as of IATA Mar 20 report

geopolitical.iranConflict: Active conflict. US-Israeli strikes on Iran Feb 28, 2026 prompted retaliation. Iran rejected direct US peace talks Mar 26 -- no near-term diplomatic resolution. Strait of Hormuz effectively closed to commercial traffic since Mar 2, disrupting ~17.8M bbl/day. No signs of de-escalation as of Mar 31.
airspaceClosures: Iran (OIIX FIR) -- CLOSED to commercial aviation
Israel (LLLL FIR) -- RESTRICTED, limited PPR only
Bahrain, UAE, Qatar -- HEAVILY RESTRICTED, short-notice closures possible
geopolitical.regionalStability: Southeast Asia airspace stable and unaffected by direct conflict. Fuel supply chains remain severely disrupted. Thailand acting as transit hub for rerouted Europe-Asia traffic. No direct security threat to SEA aviation. Vietnamese airline coordinated cuts begin TOMORROW (April 1).
details: Iranian drones struck Nakhchivan airport (Azerbaijan) on Mar 5, first conflict spillover into Caucasus corridor
Traditional Gulf corridor for Europe-Asia flights effectively unavailable indefinitely
Two rerouting corridors in use: Southern (Egypt-Saudi-Oman, +90min) and Northern (Caucasus-Central Asia, +2-3hrs)
AeroThai reports 1,000+ flights cancelled in Thailand alone due to Gulf crisis cumulative
Tourism arrivals to Thailand dropped 9% in early March, 18% decline from Europe/Middle East specifically
Iran's rejection of US peace talks on Mar 26 eliminated last near-term diplomatic off-ramp
Brent crude heading for record monthly gain of ~55% in March 2026

airasia.cancellationRate: Elevated. Among hardest-hit carriers in Mar 10 wave (184 cancellations across Asia). No fuel hedging, absorbing full fuel cost spike. Research house suspended stock call citing uncertainty.
airasia.financialHealth: Budget carrier with thin margins, no significant fuel hedging. Every $1/bbl fuel increase reduces earnings ~7%. Fare increases of 26% insufficient to fully offset the fuel shock. Affiliate revenue losses exceeding $180M for Q1.
airasia.recentDisruptions: Multiple disruption events Feb-Mar 2026. Philippines AirAsia suspended Manila-HCMC 3x weekly through May. Thai AirAsia still operating CXR-DMK daily but under increasing fuel cost pressure. Implementing fuel surcharges on key routes.

thaiAirways.cancellationRate: Near normal. 74 delays logged but managing better than most carriers. Rerouting European services with minimal schedule impact.
thaiAirways.financialHealth: Government-backed flag carrier, post-restructuring. Absorbing fuel costs via 10-15% ticket price increases. Better positioned than budget carriers to weather the crisis.
thaiAirways.recentDisruptions: BKK-CPH operating with rerouting via southern or northern corridor. Some delays at BKK due to increased transit traffic. No route cancellations for BKK-CPH reported as of Mar 31.

cxrToDmk.notes: SEA airspace unaffected by Middle East closures. Primary risk is Vietnam's jet fuel shortage, not airspace. Flight rebooked to April 13 -- departing ahead of schedule. Vietnamese airline April 1 cuts now in effect; verify FD647 on April 13 is confirmed and not affected by post-cut schedule changes. Ho Chi Minh City-Bangkok route already reduced from 12 to 9 daily flights -- Nha Trang routes face same pressure, but April 13 departure leaves 7-day visa buffer and gets out before cuts potentially deepen.

bkkToCph.notes: Traditional Gulf corridor unavailable. Thai Airways rerouting via southern (Egypt-Saudi-Oman, +90min, currently favored) or northern (Caucasus/Central Asia, +2-3hrs) corridors. Route still operating but longer and more expensive. No direct Thai Airways BKK-CPH cancellations reported as of Mar 31. Passengers should expect extended flight time.

actions.action: Verify AirAsia April 13 booking (FD647) is fully confirmed in the app -- Vietnamese airline April 1 cuts are now in effect. Check for any schedule change notifications. If April 13 shows disrupted, book VietJet or Vietnam Airlines CXR-BKK on the same date as backup.

Coordinate early checkout from current Nha Trang Airbnb (before April 13). Settle the utilities deposit with the host -- initiate the conversation early to avoid last-minute friction. Deposit settlement was needed regardless; just moving it up.

Thai Airways BKK-CPH operating with rerouting -- expect 1.5-3hr longer flight. Confirm booking is still active and check for any schedule change notifications from Thai Airways. No need to rebook yet.
If Brent rises above $120 or additional airspace closures occur, evaluate backup routing: BKK to CPH via Istanbul (Turkish Airlines) or Helsinki (Finnair) as alternatives to Thai Airways direct.

oilPriceTimeline.event: Pre-conflict baseline. Markets calm, IATA forecast $88/bbl for 2026.
Brent drops to $67.4 as US-Iran diplomatic talks ease tensions.
Markets turn bullish anticipating US military action against Iran. Brent trading ~$10 above fair value.
Last day before strikes. Oil at pre-war levels.
US-Israeli strikes on Iranian military targets. War begins. Immediate price spike.
Iranian missile/drone retaliation. Brent surges 10-13% from pre-war.
Iranian drones strike Nakhchivan airport (Azerbaijan). Brent up 15% since war began.
Thailand bans fuel exports. China restricts refined oil exports.
Peak spike. Near halt in Strait of Hormuz tanker traffic. 65% increase from Feb 27.
Partial correction. Still up 37% from pre-war. IATA warns of sudden price challenge.
IATA weekly monitor: jet fuel $197/bbl for week ending Mar 20, up 58.4% from Feb 20.
Brent eases but jet fuel stays elevated due to supply chain lag.
Iran rejects US peace talks. Prices reignite. Jet fuel up 12.6% week-on-week.
Brent closes at highest since July 2022, up 4.2% on day. Bloomberg/Japan Times: Asia air travel crisis risks spreading to Europe.
Brent holds at 4-year highs, up ~55% in March (record monthly gain). Jet fuel ticks up to $234.3/bbl. Vietnamese airline cuts take effect in 2 days.

Brent holding at $112.78. Vietnamese airline cuts take effect TOMORROW (April 1). No diplomatic progress. Goldman Sachs: $14-18 geopolitical risk premium remains. Jet fuel up 94.4% year-on-year per IATA.

cancellationTimeline.details: Normal disruption levels across SEA. Typical daily: 50-100 delays, <20 cancellations region-wide.
1,198 delays + 21 cancellations across Changi, Jakarta, Bali, Manila, Cebu. Pre-war disruptions.
4,216 delays + 62 cancellations. AirAsia alone had 247 delays. Infrastructure strain at SEA hubs.
Disruptions across Thailand, Japan, Indonesia, Vietnam, Philippines, Hong Kong. ~150-200k passengers affected.
1,400+ delays + 93 cancellations. Jakarta logged 104 delays alone, KL reported 470.
WAR BEGINS. 2,513 cancellations + 2,117 delays. Changi: 32 cancelled. BKK: 32 cancelled. Massive wave.
150-300% above normal daily disruption rates. JAL, Thai Airways, Air India, ANA, AirAsia, Lion Air all affected.
328 Thailand flights disrupted. Hundreds of passengers stranded across Asia.
1,043 delays + 184 cancellations across SEA. AirAsia among hardest-hit carriers.
AeroThai reports cumulative 1,000+ flights cancelled in Thailand alone due to Gulf crisis.
65+ international/regional flights cancelled across Indonesia, Malaysia, Hong Kong, Taiwan.
325 delays + 50 cancellations in Thailand. Thai Airways, Bangkok Airways, AirAsia, Emirates affected. + 56 cancelled in Indonesia/Malaysia.

Vietnamese airlines announce coordinated pullback from April 1. Vietnam Airlines suspending 7 domestic routes, cutting 23 weekly flights. VietJet cutting 8%. Restoration projected April 10-15 contingent on fuel shipments.

Vietnamese airline cuts take effect TOMORROW (April 1). Asia air travel crisis spreading toward Europe per Bloomberg. Fuel crisis unresolved: jet fuel at $234.3/bbl, Brent at $112.78. Brent up ~55% in March -- record monthly gain. No diplomatic progress.

seaAirlineSummary: Southeast Asian aviation under severe and sustained stress. Fuel crisis persists at critical levels: Brent $112.78, jet fuel $234.3/bbl (Mar 31). Vietnamese carriers' coordinated April cuts begin TOMORROW. Budget carriers face existential fuel economics with no hedges and fare increases insufficient to offset costs. Flag carriers managing with rerouting and price hikes but margin pressure mounting. Bloomberg warns crisis is spreading beyond Asia. Brent posted ~55% gain in March -- record monthly rise. No relief expected in April unless Middle East stabilizes.

seaAirlineOverview.name: Vietnam Airlines
seaAirlineOverview.impact: Cutting 23 weekly flights and suspending 7 domestic routes from April 1 (TOMORROW). Planning 10-20% monthly cuts if jet fuel stays above $160-200/bbl.
seaAirlineOverview.detail: Imports 60%+ of jet fuel from China and Thailand, both of which have banned exports. Fuel delivery timelines stretched from 30 to 50+ days.

seaAirlineOverview.name: VietJet Air
seaAirlineOverview.impact: Cancelling Korea-Vietnam flights en masse including Nha Trang routes from April. Reducing 8% on regional network as part of coordinated Vietnamese pullback from April 1 (TOMORROW).
seaAirlineOverview.detail: Budget model makes them highly vulnerable to 147% fuel cost increase since pre-war. Connects Vietnam to Cambodia, Thailand, Laos.

seaAirlineOverview.name: AirAsia (Group)
seaAirlineOverview.impact: Among hardest-hit in Mar 10 cancellation wave. Philippines AirAsia suspended Manila-HCMC 3x weekly through May. Research house suspended stock call. Affiliate revenue losses exceeding $180M for Q1.
seaAirlineOverview.detail: Thin margins, no significant fuel hedging. Thai AirAsia still operating CXR-DMK daily (FD647) but under increasing pressure. Every $1/bbl fuel increase reduces earnings ~7%. Ho Chi Minh City-Bangkok route cut from 12 to 9 daily flights.

seaAirlineOverview.name: Thai Airways
seaAirlineOverview.impact: Rerouting European services via southern or northern corridor with minimal schedule impact. 74 delays but managing better than most. Raised prices 10-15%.
seaAirlineOverview.detail: Government-backed, post-restructuring. Better positioned to absorb fuel costs. BKK-CPH still operating as of Mar 31.

seaAirlineOverview.name: Cebu Pacific
seaAirlineOverview.impact: Major suspensions: Davao-BKK, Iloilo-BKK, Iloilo-SGN, Clark-HAN all suspended Apr-Oct 2026. Trimmed frequencies to SIN, JKT, KUL, MEL, SYD.
seaAirlineOverview.detail: Philippines declared fuel emergency. Cebu Pacific among most aggressive in cutting routes to survive.

seaAirlineOverview.name: Bangkok Airways
seaAirlineOverview.impact: Implementing fuel surcharges of 1,200-2,000 baht. No major route suspensions reported yet.
seaAirlineOverview.detail: Smaller regional carrier. Boutique positioning may help retain premium passengers willing to pay surcharges.

seaAirlineOverview.name: Nok Air
seaAirlineOverview.impact: Implementing fuel surcharges of 1,200-2,000 baht alongside Bangkok Airways. Domestic Thai routes under pressure.
seaAirlineOverview.detail: Low-cost subsidiary model. Already operating on thin margins before the crisis.

seaAirlineOverview.name: Lion Air
seaAirlineOverview.impact: Part of broader early-March disruption wave. Indonesian domestic market under fuel pressure.
seaAirlineOverview.detail: Limited specific data, but Indonesian carriers facing similar fuel supply constraints.

broaderContext.narrative: The Iran war has triggered a cascading energy crisis across Southeast Asia. Fuel prices have roughly doubled from pre-conflict levels and remain elevated: jet fuel at $234.3/bbl (Mar 31), Brent at $112.78 -- on track for a record ~55% monthly gain in March. The coordinated Vietnamese airline pullback takes effect TOMORROW (April 1) -- carriers are no longer just reacting to disruptions but planning sustained reductions. Bloomberg and Japan Times are warning the crisis is spreading beyond Asia toward Europe. Iran's rejection of peace talks on Mar 26 removed the last near-term diplomatic off-ramp. The situation has been stable-but-elevated since late March, with no new escalation but no resolution either.

policies.description: PM Order 2/2026: Banned exports of gasoline, gasohol, diesel, Jet A-1 aviation fuel, and LPG (except to Cambodia and Laos). Froze diesel prices. Required large fuel traders (100k+ tonnes/yr) to increase reserves to 1.5-3%.

Diesel shortage crisis deepening. Long queues at fuel stations in provinces. PM denied large-scale hoarding, says everyday users panic-buying (~17M litres/day). Government considering revival of 1973 emergency energy law.
Banned refined fuel exports, including jet fuel. Directly cut off ~60% of Vietnam's jet fuel supply. China prioritizing domestic energy security over regional export commitments.
Declared fuel emergency. Government moved to 4-day work week for government offices. Philippine carriers (Cebu Pacific, Philippines AirAsia) among most aggressive in cutting routes.

Coordinated airline pullback taking effect April 1. Vietnam Airlines suspending 7 domestic routes, cutting 23 weekly flights. VietJet reducing 8%. Officials encouraged to work from home. Fuel delivery timelines 50+ days. Gasoline taxes removed, cutting pump price 19%.
Military junta introduced odds-and-evens driving system (fuel rationing). Alternating driving days based on license plate numbers.

broaderContext.outlook: Aviation disruption will persist through Q2 2026 unless Middle East stability improves. Strait of Hormuz remains effectively closed (~17.8M bbl/day disrupted). Iran's rejection of peace talks on Mar 26 removed near-term diplomatic hope. Oil in backwardation signals continued tightness. Brent up ~55% in March -- record monthly gain. For SEA, the China/Thailand fuel export bans are the critical chokepoint with no sign of lifting. Vietnamese carriers' April 1 cuts begin TOMORROW. Partial schedule restoration projected April 10-15 -- contingent on fuel shipments actually arriving. Bloomberg warning the crisis is spreading to Europe.
